questions.q: What is the legendary bird that is consumed by fire and reborn from its own ashes?
questions.explanation: The Phoenix is a mythical bird symbolizing rebirth and immortality through its cycle of death and renewal. The Roc is a giant bird, harpies are winged demons, and Pegasus is a flying horse.

questions.q: In various cultures' mythology, what massive sea creature is depicted as a giant squid or octopus?
questions.explanation: The Kraken is the legendary giant sea creature feared by sailors, depicted as a massive cephalopod. Leviathan is a sea monster in Judeo-Christian tradition, the Hydra is a multi-headed serpent, and dragons vary across cultures.

questions.q: What is the name of the three-headed dog that guards the gates of the underworld in Greek mythology?
questions.explanation: Cerberus is the three-headed hound guarding Hades' entrance. The Sphinx asks riddles, the Chimera is a fire-breathing monster with multiple animal parts, and the Manticore is a hybrid predator.

questions.q: What horned, magical horse is associated with purity and grace in medieval European mythology?
questions.explanation: The Unicorn is the single-horned horse symbolizing purity in medieval European tradition. Pegasus is a winged horse, the Hippogriff is a griffon-like creature, and the Kirin is an Asian chimera.

questions.q: In Greek mythology, what is the monster with the head of a man and the body of a bull?
questions.explanation: The Minotaur has the head of a bull and body of a man, imprisoned in a labyrinth. Medusa was a woman with snakes for hair, satyrs are half-man half-goat, and gorgons are women with serpents.

questions.q: What dragon in Norse mythology is prophesied to break free and bring about the end of the world?
questions.explanation: Jörmungandr is the world serpent in Norse mythology that breaks free at Ragnarok. Fafnir is a dragon slain by Sigurd, Nidhogg gnaws at Yggdrasil's roots, and Smaug is from modern fiction.

questions.q: In various Asian mythologies, what benevolent, scaled, horned creature is associated with water and good fortune?
questions.explanation: The Dragon in Asian mythology is a benevolent, divine creature associated with water, luck, and prosperity. Phoenixes represent rebirth, tigers are fierce, and cranes symbolize longevity.

questions.q: What hybrid creature has the head and wings of an eagle and the body of a lion?
questions.explanation: A Griffon (or Griffin) combines an eagle's head and wings with a lion's body, symbolizing strength and vigilance. The Hippogriff adds a horse element, the Chimera has multiple different animals, and Pegasus is purely equine.

questions.q: In Greek mythology, what goddess was transformed into a monster with snakes for hair whose gaze turns mortals to stone?
questions.explanation: Medusa was transformed into a gorgon with venomous snakes instead of hair and a petrifying gaze by Athena. Athena was the goddess who cursed her, Hera is the queen of gods, and Circe is a witch.

questions.q: What serpentine monster in Greek mythology had multiple heads that regrew when cut off?
questions.explanation: The Hydra was a serpent with multiple heads; cutting one off would cause two to regrow, making it nearly immortal. Python was a dragon guarding Delphi, the Basilisk is a later creature, and the Chimera was a different monster.
